channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Firing the CEO is the wrong answer, ask Sean McDermott
item.description: The annual NFL tradition of firing the head coach as the season ends continues. This year, 10 top coaches got the axe, a staggering 31% of all NFL coa...

item.title: From mom-and-pops to Goldman Sachs, Jesse Jackson’s vision for American business sparked a revolution
item.description: The civil rights leader, whose dealmaking skills pushed Wall Street to invest in minority-owned businesses, died on Tuesday.

item.title: At AI summit, Prime Minister Modi pitches India as global hub for artificial intelligence
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Thursday pitched India as a central player in the global artificial intelligence ecosystem, saying the country ...

item.title: Amazon Created a Huge Hiring Mess That Other Companies Will Pay For
item.description: The over-scrutinizing of job candidates has reached new levels of stupid.

item.title: For some job seekers, Tinder is the new LinkedIn
item.description: They say job hunting is just like dating. Some are taking that advice literally. “Job market so bad I’m using Hinge to find work,” one job seeker post...

item.title: Michael Jordan’s Hardest Win Didn’t Happen on a Basketball Court. Here’s What Leading in a New Industry Looks Like
item.description: When you move from operator to architect, your leverage changes.

item.title: Researchers Tested 81 Popular Headphones for Toxic Chemicals. Every Single One Tested Positive
item.description: The study detected hormone-disrupting plastics in nearly every pair.

item.title: Corporate Marketers Are Quitting—and Building Powerful New Consulting Models Instead
item.description: High-performing marketers are redesigning how income, influence, and career security work.

item.title: American Express and Visa alumni raise $4 million for Rhythmic, which partners with brands to provide financial products for their customers
item.description: Startup aims to make stablecoins more accessible to the general public.

item.title: The Real Lesson From Lindsey Vonn’s Olympic Crash Has Nothing to Do With Risk or Resilience
item.description: Should skier Lindsey Vonn have competed while injured? Only she can say, and that’s the real lesson here.

item.title: It’s no time to go quiet on sustainability. Just ask beauty giant L’Oreal
item.description: L’Oréal is betting millions that sustainability can drive profits as well as protect the planet.

item.title: Trump’s tariffs are a ‘dirty tax’ that will make the $38.6 trillion national debt crisis even worse over the long term, top analyst says
item.description: "The idea that this is pro-American is actually just the opposite," Penn Wharton's Kent Smetters told Fortune. "It hurts American manufacturers."

item.title: Google co-founder Sergey Brin’s unretirement is a lesson for the rest of us
item.description: When Sergey Brin spoke at Stanford University’s school of engineering centennial celebration recently, the Google co-founder was open about his career...

item.title: This Fortune 500 CEO says ‘lean manufacturing’—the management philosophy Toyota made famous—is a prerequisite for leveraging AI
item.description: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.

item.title: 5 reasons AI-assisted coding could break your business
item.description: AI is helping teams build software and tools faster than ever—but that doesn’t mean we’re building smarter. I’ve seen entire prototypes spin up in a d...

item.title: A stock-market strategy that combines value, quality and momentum
U.S. first-time jobless claims fall to lowest level so far this year
Etsy on track for 20% rally after sale of Depop unit to eBay
U.S.’s trade deficit for 2025 was its third biggest ever
Your stocks are becoming 24/7 digital cash
